title: Improving Verification of Battery Cell Monitoring Chips - Semiwiki
gist: A Q&A with Dukosi verification lead Anders Convery details a contactless, per-cell battery monitoring architecture (Cell Monitor IC + System Hub IC using proprietary C-SynQ near-field RF) and how AMIQ EDA's DVT IDE and Verissimo linter accelerate SystemVerilog/UVM verification to meet ASIL D / ISO 26262 automotive safety standards.

insights.claim: Dukosi's next-generation battery cell monitoring is a two-chip solution—a Cell Monitor IC installed on each cell providing voltage and temperature measurements, and a System Hub IC—communicating via its proprietary C-SynQ protocol over near-field RF with deterministic latency.
insights.speaker: Tom Anderson
insights.so_what: Per-cell contactless monitoring is a differentiated architecture for EV BMS; a signal on where battery safety electronics are heading and a competitive threat to wired module designs.
insights.quote: One Cell Monitor chip is installed on each cell in the battery pack
insights.locator: I'm the verification lead for the digital portions

insights.claim: Traditional wired battery systems typically measure temperature at only one or two points per module (with 12-24 cells per module in an EV battery), whereas Dukosi's per-cell on-cell architecture provides far higher data granularity for identifying performance or safety issues.
insights.speaker: Tom Anderson
insights.so_what: Quantifies the granularity gap that justifies the new architecture; higher monitoring resolution is a safety/differentiation lever for EV battery suppliers.
insights.quote: traditional approaches usually only measure temperature at one or two points in each module, with typically 12-24 cells per module in an EV battery

insights.claim: Dukosi's contactless architecture eliminates the cabling and fault-prone connectors found in traditional battery solutions, which the company frames as enabling more robust, proactive battery management.
insights.speaker: Tom Anderson
insights.so_what: Removing connectors attacks a known EV failure mode and BOM cost/weight; relevant to EV reliability and warranty-cost narratives.
insights.quote: Our contactless communication and architecture eliminate the cabling and fault prone connectors found in traditional solutions

insights.claim: Dukosi's chips split silicon roughly evenly between analog (sensors, RF transceivers) and highly complex digital content including multiple processor cores, sophisticated power management, and fault protection logic with many corner cases.
insights.speaker: Tom Anderson
insights.so_what: The mixed-signal, corner-case-heavy design explains why verification tooling is a bottleneck—context for EDA demand in safety-critical automotive silicon.
insights.quote: The analog portions... take up about the same amount of silicon real estate as the digital.
insights.locator: It seems as if your chips must have a mix

insights.claim: Dukosi must satisfy the Automotive SPICE (ASPICE) maturity model and ASIL D as defined by the ISO 26262 functional safety standard, embedding zero-bug quality requirements into its product roadmap and quality process.
insights.speaker: Tom Anderson
insights.so_what: ASIL D is the highest automotive safety integrity level; compliance is a gating requirement and a moat for suppliers who can meet it—key qualifier for automotive design wins.
insights.quote: we must satisfy the Automotive SPICE (ASPICE) maturity model and Automotive Safety Integrity Level D (ASIL D)
insights.locator: What are some of your challenges?

themes.theme: AMIQ EDA tooling and verification ROI

insights.claim: Dukosi has used AMIQ EDA's DVT IDE for seven to eight years across two chip generations and added the Verissimo SystemVerilog Linter for the current project without evaluating alternatives, citing no competitor that looked as good.
insights.speaker: Tom Anderson
insights.so_what: Sticky, multi-generation tool adoption with no competitive eval signals strong switching costs and account lock-in for AMIQ EDA in the verification tool niche.
insights.quote: We had a brief look at alternatives but didn't see anything that looked as good as Verissimo.

insights.claim: The verification lead states that without Verissimo his team's efficiency would slow down fourfold, and that catching a linting bug at write-time takes minutes versus roughly a full day when bugs slipped through to simulation debugging.
insights.speaker: Tom Anderson
insights.so_what: A concrete 4x productivity and minutes-vs-day ROI claim quantifies the value proposition for shift-left verification tooling—useful for sizing EDA productivity gains.
insights.quote: Without it, my efficiency would slow down fourfold.
insights.locator: Anything else you'd like to add?

insights.claim: Dukosi runs Verissimo on all check-ins integrated into its CI/CD flow, but does not require a clean run, so it functions as a soft gate rather than a hard one—engineers introducing new linting errors must make a conscious, justified decision.
insights.speaker: Tom Anderson
insights.so_what: Illustrates a pragmatic CI/CD integration pattern for verification linting; relevant to how EDA linters embed into dev workflows and drive recurring usage.
insights.quote: We do run Verissimo on all check-ins. We don't require a clean run, so it's not a hard gate.
insights.locator: Do you use Verissimo to gate

insights.claim: Dukosi configured the Verissimo rule set with AMIQ EDA to capture nearly all its SystemVerilog/UVM coding guidelines in alignment with ASPICE PAM 4.0 and ISO 26262, solving the challenge of enforcing standards manually especially when using contractors during peak development.
insights.speaker: Tom Anderson
insights.so_what: Configurable rule-set customization to safety standards deepens tool stickiness and shows how compliance requirements pull verification tooling into automotive design.
insights.locator: Do you have SystemVerilog and UVM coding guidelines?

insights.claim: Verissimo catches issues humans miss in review—duplicated code, dead code, outdated coverage groups that become coverage holes, and unchecked function/task returns or failed casts.
insights.speaker: Tom Anderson
insights.so_what: Concrete examples of defect classes automated linting removes from manual review—supports the shift-left value case for verification automation.

insights.claim: Dukosi has not yet adopted AMIQ EDA's AI Assistant, has only partially adopted Specador Documentation Generator, and reports its design team has not fully adopted Verissimo, indicating headroom for further tool expansion.
insights.speaker: Tom Anderson
insights.so_what: Signals expansion/upsell runway within an existing account—relevant to AMIQ EDA's land-and-expand growth trajectory.
insights.quote: We have not looked at AI Assistant yet, so that's on our list to investigate as well.
insights.locator: How do you plan to improve
